title: Dendrolirium latilabellum
taxonomy: Dendrolirium latilabellum belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which comprises the largest and most diverse group of flowering plants. This epiphytic orchid is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Dendrobieae. More specifically, it is placed in the genus Dendrolirium, which is native to Southeast Asia. The classification of Dendrolirium latilabellum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other orchid species. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of this orchid species.

properties: Dendrolirium latilabellum is known for its potential therapeutic properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, headache, and respiratory problems. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. constituents: Dendrolirium latilabellum contains flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are active constituents found in the plant. intro: Dendrolirium latilabellum, also known as Dendrobium latilabellum, is a species of orchid native to China, characterized by its unique, cylindrical pseudobulbs and fragrant white flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. Bioactive constituents of Dendrobium latilabellum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Dendrobium latilabellum is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is often prepared as a decoction or tea, as well as being formulated into capsules or tablets for convenience.
